sn9.3:1.1: On one occasion Ven. Kassapa Gotta was staying among the Kosalans in a forest grove.
sn9.3:1.2: Now at that time, as he had gone to spend the day (in the grove), he was exhorting a certain hunter.
sn9.3:1.3: Then the devatā inhabiting the forest grove, feeling sympathy for Ven. Kassapa Gotta, desiring his benefit, desiring to bring him to his senses, approached him and recited verses:
sn9.3:2.4: roaming through the rugged mountains, undiscerning, unaware.
sn9.3:3.4: the fool doesn’t awaken to the meaning.
sn9.3:4.1: Even if you were to bring
sn9.3:4.4: for his eyes can’t be found.”
sn9.3:5.1: Then Ven. Kassapa Gotta, chastened by the devatā, came to his senses.
